task_completion.description: Navigate to the Comm Links settings in QGroundControl.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the QGroundControl application open with the 'Application Settings' -> 'Comm Links' tab visible.

task_completion.description: Configure a new TCP communication link with the specified parameters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Comm Link configuration panel in QGroundControl showing Name: 'LTE_Field_Relay', Type: 'TCP', Host Address: '127.0.0.1', and Port: '5762'.

task_completion.description: Connect to the newly created TCP link.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'LTE_Field_Relay' link is selected in the Comm Links list and shows as connected (e.g., the button says 'Disconnect' instead of 'Connect', or the link is highlighted as active).

task_completion.description: Disable UDP autoconnect in QGroundControl settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In QGroundControl Application Settings, under the 'AutoConnect' section, the checkbox for 'UDP' is unchecked.

task_completion.description: Ensure the vehicle remains connected after the configuration changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The top bar of QGroundControl displays active vehicle telemetry (e.g., flight mode, battery level, GPS status), indicating a successful connection to the drone/vehicle.

task_completion.description: Write the connection verification report with all required details.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing the file '/home/ga/Documents/QGC/connection_report.txt'. The text includes the link name ('LTE_Field_Relay'), type ('TCP'), host ('127.0.0.1'), port ('5762'), the detected vehicle type, a statement confirming UDP autoconnect is disabled, and a sign-off statement.

integrity.description: The agent used the QGroundControl GUI to configure the link and settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through QGroundControl menus and dialogs, rather than exclusively editing the QGroundControl.ini file via terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely checked the vehicle type in QGC before writing the report.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent viewed the main QGroundControl vehicle view or vehicle setup pages to identify the vehicle type, rather than guessing or hallucinating it in the text editor.

integrity.description: The agent manually created and edited the report file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a text editor (like gedit, nano, or vim) being used to write the report, rather than a single 'echo' command pasting a pre-written block of text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected TCP link name is 'LTE_Field_Relay' on port 5762. The report must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/QGC/connection_report.txt' and include the link name, type (TCP), host (127.0.0.1), port (5762), detected vehicle type, confirmation of disabled UDP autoconnect, and a sign-off statement.
